title: Creepy Twins, Religious Fanaticism and a Loaded Gun Collide in Tense Chamber Drama ‘Godhead’ — Fantasia
full_text: Jul 23, 2026 6:41pm PT Creepy Twins, Religious Fanaticism and a Loaded Gun Collide in Tense Chamber Drama ‘Godhead’ — Fantasia By William Earl Plus Icon William Earl @beautifulbill Latest Creepy Twins, Religious Fanaticism and a Loaded Gun Collide in Tense Chamber Drama ‘Godhead’ — Fantasia 8 hours ago ‘I’ve Been Scammed Left and Right’: Demand for ‘Odyssey’ Tickets Gives Rise to Sketchy Scalpers 3 days ago Variety Interview Studio Presented by Google TV Returns to San Diego 4 days ago See All Courtesy of Fantasia Festival Mark H. Rapaport’s new film “ Godhead ” is a master-class in ratcheting up tension. Shot in black-and-white with camerawork that makes the audience feel like a voyeur uncovering perverse secrets, the film blends faith, sex, lies and discomfort into an unknowable dance between its leads. The premise begins simply: A rural Catholic priest (Al Warren) is visited by two bizarre twins (Sarah Coffey and Kimball Farley), who look like an unholy blend of Die Antwoord, Grimes and overcooked ravers. They believe they are part of a prophecy and need the priest’s help to fulfill it. They also have a gun. And the priest has a young boy (Luke Speakman) that he seems to keep captive indoors … Related Stories Sky Yang Signs With Range Media Partners (EXCLUSIVE) To say more would ruin the film’s twists and turns, which were met enthusiastically at its world premiere screenings at Montreal’s genre-focused Fantasia Festival this week. Popular on Variety Rapaport, whose well-received 2023 debut feature “Hippo” also premiered at Fantasia, spoke with Variety about his choice to do another film in black and white, how “Godhead” takes aim at crooked authority figures and leaving the audience with challenging questions. What inspired you to develop this story? I always find that in life I come up against authority figures in a way that feels unfair. I often am too nice to people, and I think I have a little bit of second-born’s gullibility. As I grow up and hopefully become a dad, I’m trying to shed that. I think in this movie, I was able to put a finger on that feeling of being aware of the hypocrite or the manipulator. From childhood until adulthood, you have your religious figures, you have your family, you have your bosses. I’m always a very trusting person, and so I found that there’s real horror in that. This movie, although framed as a Hitchcockian thriller of sorts, is really a canvas for me to explore those frustrations. With a film like this that has a twisty plot, how are you able to balance disseminating information to the audience without overexplaining and giving them easy answers? It’s a tough tightrope to walk. I hope I’m able to do that successfully by staying with the characters first and being able to feel with them, and writing the script from their intentions and their heart, and not letting the plot of a horror film take over the greater emotional core. Of course, we do go there and go to dark places and twist and turn, but I try to always bring it back to the characters. I always can do better finding that humanity. There are plenty of dark moments in this film. How did you determine how bleak versus hopeful you ultimately wanted your work to be? It’s funny, there was another version of the ending where it was a massive, obvious F-you to all of these evil characters. I was talking to Al Warren, who plays the priest beautifully and with so much real emotion, and he got me thinking about the end beat. Without spoiling it, he said, “Let’s think about where this could go if you, as the director, practiced forgiveness,” and I found that in doing that I actually ended up with a much more horrifying ending than had I brought the hammer down on his character. I think there’s an eeriness to forgiveness as well, in that the ultimate manipulation is forgiving your manipulator. I wouldn’t mind if the audience felt maybe characters made the wrong decision at the end. That just keeps the question going, because the movie at its core is a question for the audience, less than an answer. In my old version, I tried to provide the answer, and it just wasn’t satisfying. So it was a real exercise in reeling back that sword that the director wields. That’s where we ended up. What was the creative choice to shoot in black and white instead of color? It was my instinct off the bat just because my first film was in black and white and it was such a fun, beautiful little film. People loved the black and white. It felt like a different world. And so it started as a, “Well, if it’s not broke, don’t fix it.” My brain was thinking in black and white. But of course, you need the narrative to deserve black and white. As I started to write the script, I just couldn’t see it any other way. I was very much into Robert Eggers’ “The Lighthouse.” He makes a ton of great movies, but this three-hander … I wanted to place us in some sort of timeless world. It felt black and white was an effective way to do that. In addition to the beauty of the shadows and the moodiness that it’s able to convey, there’s how it relates to the themes of good and evil, right and wrong, truth and untruth. I had a full-circle moment in writing the script where there’s a gray area that I put in there, because, even though it’s in black and white, it potentially concludes on a gray area, which to me is the most horrifying area of all. We all want to know: Are we black or white? Are we right or wrong? When I was able to think of it as a spectrum and not just black and white, it opened up a whole new visual language for me. Alief is representing “Godhead” for worldwide sales as it searches for distribution. Watch the “Godhead” teaser trailer below. title: Zach Cregger Teases His ‘Weapons’ Prequel and Its “Crazy” Approach to Gladys’ Origin Story
full_text: × Skip to main content By Ryan Gajewski Plus Icon Ryan Gajewski Senior Entertainment Reporter View All July 23, 2026 6:30pm Amy Madigan in 'Weapons.' Quantrell Colbert/Warner Bros. Logo text Zach Cregger is ready to work his magic with the prequel to Weapons that will focus on breakout character Gladys. During Collider’ s Directors on Directing panel at San Diego Comic-Con on Thursday, the filmmaker shared a status update on the planned feature follow-up to his 2025 horror hit that won Amy Madigan the best supporting actress Oscar for her role as Aunt Gladys. The Hollywood Reporter previously reported that Cregger and Zach Shields are penning the script for the project that is in development at New Line Cinema with the working title Gladys . Related Stories TV Peacock's 'Dungeon Crawler Carl' Series Casts Audiobook Narrator Jeff Hays Movies Johnny Depp Surprises Comic-Con as Ebenezer Scrooge on Streets of San Diego: "It's Good to Be Back" “First of all, I’m really excited about the Gladys movie, and I loved working with Amy,” Cregger told the crowd. “I loved that character. For the movie Weapons , it really made a lot of sense not to dive into her backstory because the device of Weapons was to keep her very mysterious.” He added, “However, there’s an amazing story to be told about where Gladys comes from and how she became what we know her as. It’s got a lot of twists and turns and mysteries and crazy things, like Weapons does. I think it’s going to be great. The script is in progress now, but we’re taking that super seriously, and it’s moving in the right direction. So yeah, it’s being written, is the short answer.” Centering on a group of children who go missing in the middle of the night, Weapons hit theaters last August from Warner Bros. and collected $270 million at the global box office. In addition to Madigan, the cast included Julia Garner, Josh Brolin, Alden Ehrenreich, Austin Abrams and Benedict Wong. At Thursday’s panel, Cregger screened the latest trailer and additional exclusive footage from his forthcoming video game adaptation Resident Evil , which Sony releases in September. Cregger noted that his next feature will be The Flood , a sci-fi film that he wrote for New Line. Also participating in the panel were Mayday directors John Francis Daley and Jonathan Goldstein , who teased the progress on the new Star Trek feature that the pair are writing and directing . “It won’t be the characters you’ve seen previously,” Goldstein said from the stage. “It will be a little further into the future than what you’ve seen.” THR

title: ‘Stuart Fails To Save The Universe’ Creators & Stars On Premiere’s Shocking OG Death & Its Aftermath
full_text: Skip to main content By Nellie Andreeva Nellie Andreeva Editor-In-Chief @DeadlineNellie More Stories By Nellie ‘Stuart Fails To Save The Universe’ Creators & Stars On Premiere’s Shocking OG Death & Its Aftermath ‘Stuart Fails To Save The Universe’ Panel Teases DC Episode, OG Returns As ‘Big Bang Theory’ Franchise Makes Comic-Con Return Zosia Mamet Joins HBO’s ‘Industry’ For Season 5 View All July 23, 2026 6:30pm Story Arc 'Stuart Fails To Save the Universe' HBO Max SPOILER ALERT: The story includes details about the premiere episode of HBO Max’s Stuart Fails To Save the Universe . The Big Bang Theory spinoff Stuart Fails To Save the Universe starts off with Stuart (Kevin Sussman) in a post-apocalyptic Pasadena getting a visit from an alternate version of himself who urges him to restore reality after he had broken a quantum interference device built by Sheldon and Leonard, accidentally bringing about a multiverse Armageddon. The mission begins with a quest to break into CalTech to retrieve the machine. On the way there, Stuart and Bert ( Brian Posehn ) bump into Stuart’s old flame Denise ( Lauren Lapkus ) — now a common thief — and her boyfriend Gary (Tommy Walker) who decide to help them. Related Stories News 'Stuart Fails To Save The Universe' EPs & Composer On Show's Ever-Changing Title Sequence You "Shouldn't Skip", Packed With Messages & Spoilers News 'Stuart Fails To Save The Universe' Panel Teases DC Episode, OG Returns As 'Big Bang Theory' Franchise Makes Comic-Con Return Gary is swiftly (and meaninglessly) killed per the spoiler in the title sequence — read all about that and the show’s unusual opening credits here — but there was a bigger death waiting inside. Stuart, Bert and Denise are captured and imprisoned alongside Raj (original Big Bang star Kunal Nayyar ) who had been locked up there for years under the orders of “the evil one,” the Great Khalif of South Pasadena, who turns out to be none other than Big Bang ‘s Barry Kripke ( John Ross Bowie ). After some action hero heroics, Stuart, Bert, Denise and Raj get to the device and Raj is seconds from fixing it when he is shot dead in the back by Kripke, determined to protect his reign. Deadline asked Stuart Fails to Save the Universe creators Chuck Lorre, Bill Prady and Zak Penn about the shocking development involving a legacy Big Bang character. “Well, he’s only gone in this one universe, as Kripke points out in the next universe,” Lorre and Prady said. Added Penn, “We liked the idea, I think we all thought it would be fun to do because we knew that we could bring them back,” he said of Raj and other OG Big Bang characters. “But I think it’ll be fun for people watching the first episode, don’t you? That there’ll be this moment of like, oh my God, they killed Raj!” During Stuart Fails To Save the Universe ‘s Thursday Comic-Com panel that followed a screening of the premiere, Prady teased more Big Bang returns . “You saw one wonderful member of the the Big Bang Theory family in that first episode,” he said, referring to Nayyar. “There are more, and there are also some performers who are some wonderful minor characters that are coming back.” Speaking to Deadline, Bowie addressed the evil turn Kripke takes in the premiere, capped by the coldblooded murder of Raj. “I would be very curious as to how America reacts to that, I gotta say,” he said. “It moves the plot forward, and in the very next episode, he makes the point that maybe that universe doesn’t exist anymore, in which case, I did not kill Raj, or I killed one of an infinite number of Rajes, so it is after all a ‘wounding ewwow’ — which is harder to say than it sounds.” As you can see in the video of his comments below, Bowie slipped into character, saying “rounding error” with Kripke’s signature rhotacism speech pattern. “So they kind of rationalize it, but that said, Kunal is the sweetest, he is made of puppies, basically, and to have to shoot him in the back, no less, didn’t feel great, I won’t lie to you,” Boiwe added. “But then immediately, what happens? Burt kicks my ass in the most extended fight scene either of us has ever done. Burt hands me my ass immediately following — which was apparently Zak Penn’s idea — like, if he’s going to do this, he has to immediately have his comeuppance.” Bowie got a lot of flak from fans — and his co-stars — for what his character did to Raj during the Stuart Fails To Save the Universe ‘s Comic-Com panel. “He’s the most likable character maybe in TV history, it’s like shooting Fonzie, I feel terrible,” Bowie said of Raj before reassuring the audience, “I only do it once. Sorry, I will spoil that.” Later, Bowie was teased again, this time by Posehn, over the killing. “You saw that Raj comes back, right? I really wanted them to show that Raj comes back, or else I wasn’t going to come out here,” he told his co-stars, adding, “Why couldn’t I have just shot Howard?” That last line — referencing one of Big Bang ‘s main characters, Kripke’s engineering rival Howard Wolowitz (Simon Helberg) — got the entire Hall H laughing. *SPOILER ALERT! Do not listen if you haven't watched Episode 1 of 'Stuart Fails To Save The Universe' Bill Prady teases more 'Big Bang Theory' fan favorites will return for #StuartFailsToSaveTheUniverse And John Ross Bowie chimes in on Kripke's role in Raj's shocking death in… pic.twitter.com/Zc9xKaObxk — Deadline (@DEADLINE) July 24, 2026 As Penn explained to Deadline , Kripke was added to Stuart Fails To Save the Universe by Prady to serve as an antagonist in the mold of Dr. Zachary Smith from Lost In Space . “Obviously, he’s sort of the in-house villain of the show — much as he was on the last program — but the necessity of this group is that he eventually has to learn how to work with other people,” Bowie said at Comic-Con. “So he’s actually got a cool arc over the course of the 10 episodes. He’s Kripke, don’t get me wrong, you’re going to hear some awful stuff from Kripke’s mouth. But he has a little bit of an arc, and it’s fun to take him on that journey.” *SPOILER ALERT! Do not listen if you haven't watched Episode 1 of 'Stuart Fails To Save The Universe' John Ross Bowie on Kripke being the "in house villain" of #StuartFailsToSaveTheUniverse and his role in an OG 'Big Bang Theory' character's (Raj portrayed by Kunal Nayyar)… pic.twitter.com/whjH7w2KZH — Deadline (@DEADLINE) July 24, 2026 Indeed, while the character went very dark in the premiere, there is always a path to redemption. As Kripke says in a subsequent episode, “Being an asshole is a hobby, not a vocation” in one of the season’s more memorable lines. “I love that line too,” Bowie told Deadline. “That was really fun to say, it was a really fun moment for me too.” While he appears sympathetic to Kripke, there is one thing Bowie would change about his character. “I would love for Kripke to be just a little taller,” Bowie, 5’8″, quipped. title: ‘Stuart Fails To Save The Universe’ EPs & Composer On Show’s Ever-Changing Title Sequence You “Shouldn’t Skip”, Packed With Messages & Spoilers
full_text: Skip to main content By Nellie Andreeva Nellie Andreeva Editor-In-Chief @DeadlineNellie More Stories By Nellie ‘Stuart Fails To Save The Universe’ EPs & Composer On Show’s Ever-Changing Title Sequence You “Shouldn’t Skip”, Packed With Messages & Spoilers ‘Stuart Fails To Save The Universe’ Creators & Stars On Premiere’s Shocking OG Death & Its Aftermath ‘Stuart Fails To Save The Universe’ Panel Teases DC Episode, OG Returns As ‘Big Bang Theory’ Franchise Makes Comic-Con Return View All July 23, 2026 6:30pm Story Arc 'Stuart Fails to Save The Universe' HBO Max SPOILER ALERT: The story includes a detail about the premiere episode of HBO Max’s Stuart Fails To Save the Universe . Each episode of The Big Bang Theory spinoff Stuart Fails To Save the Universe tells a story of its four protagonists, Stuart ( Kevin Sussman ), Denise ( Lauren Lapkus ), Bert ( Brian Posehn ) and Kripke ( John Ross Bowie ), traveling to another alternate reality as they try to fix the device built by Sheldon and Leonard and the multiverse Armageddon it created. Each episode’s opening credits also tell their own story via a series of fourth wall-busting amusing messages to the viewer embedded into the animated title sequence set to a differently arranged theme song by Danny Elfman. The series of notes are capped by a spoiler, which in the Episode 1 credits reads “Barry Dies.” Related Stories News 'Stuart Fails To Save The Universe' Creators & Stars On Premiere's Shocking OG Death & Its Aftermath News 'Stuart Fails To Save The Universe' Panel Teases DC Episode, OG Returns As 'Big Bang Theory' Franchise Makes Comic-Con Return Who is Barry? He turned out to be Denise’s boyfriend in post-apocalyptic Pasadena who dies pretty meaninglessly when he says “Follow me!” and charges first into the heavily guarded CalTech, leading Stuart and Bert, joined by Denise, in their mission to break in and take Sheldon and Leonard’s machine. While the title sequence spoiled one death, the series’ premiere featured a bigger, more shocking killing of an original Big Bang character, Raj (Kunal Nayyar), who had been imprisoned at CalTech by Barry Kripke (Bowie). You can read all about it here . Deadline asked Stuart Fails To Save the Universe creators Chuck Lorre, Bill Prady and Zak Penn about the clever, custom title sequences. DEADLINE: Was this about one of you being really unhappy with the streaming culture of people skipping opening credits? LORRE : Me. PRADY : Chuck said people make these brilliant opening credit sequences that people watch one time. LORRE : And then you can click on the button that says ‘Skip Titles’, and I went, “Well, it’s a terrible waste of time and money and creativity. What if we made a title sequence that you could skip but you shouldn’t? And that would mean every title sequence have to be different and worth watching. And then we got a gift from the gods when Danny Elfman said yes, I’ll write a theme and produce a theme song, and he rearranged it and and remixed it and did variations on it throughout the 10 episodes as well, so the end result was a title sequence that hopefully is surprising and fun to watch. DEADLINE: And the spoiler part, where did it come from? Chuck, was it you again? Because some of them are really spoilers; ‘Barry Dies’ is a real spoiler. PENN : Yeah, that was I think something we all liked the idea of right off the bat is, why not? Who cares? We were like, let’s try it. Let’s just see what happens if we put a spoiler in the title, and we were just trying to keep ourselves entertained and do things that were different. PRADY : I mean, not much more than it made us laugh, the notion that, especially in the online television watching culture, there’s all of this stuff about spoilers, people hunting for spoilers, people trying to avoid spoilers. What if there’s a spoiler and it’s just sort of kind of meaningless? PENN : Also, I always felt like if we say “Spoiler: Gary Dies,” a lot of people are going to think, well, It’s not going to be that simple. So it would still be shocking when he actually does die. I’m counting on people being cynical about it. LORRE : I’m counting on people laughing. PRADY : The answer to a lot of questions is because it made us laugh, and we hope it makes other people laugh. DEADLINE: Some of the spoilers are really mild, but this one was like, first: who’s Gary?, and then it was that simple, he just died. PRADY : He died, didn’t he? And he really just died. PENN : Yeah, it’s just totally pointless. LORRE : “Follow me.” PRADY: “Follow me.” Oh. Musical Variations The Simpsons theme composer Elfman recalled “the crazy thing” that happened after he was asked to write main title music for Stuart Fails To Save the Universe and, as a Big Bang fan, agreed to do it. “I wrote it, Chuck loved it, and he said, ‘So here’s the thing. I need you to do 10 variations on it,'” Elfman said. “I thought about it for two seconds. No one’d ever asked me to do that before but I just said, ‘You got it.'” And he did it, drawing inspiration from the particular episode for its unique theme music. “It wasn’t even difficult,” he said. “Each episode, just mix it up and try things. It was really just fun.” Danny Elfman recalls how Chuck Lorre requested 10 different variations of the 'Stuart Fails to Save the Universe' theme: "No one's ever asked me to do that before" pic.twitter.com/MmlmZScV7o — Deadline (@DEADLINE) July 22, 2026 Judge Extends TRO, Pausing Paramount-WBD Merger By Another Two Weeks Surprise Depp Visit As Scrooge For ‘Ebenezer’; Thursday Photo Gallery; Panels Atari, Universal Ink Film Deal For ‘Missile Command,’ ‘Asteroids’, Other Classic Games Lineup, Talking Points, Q&A With Festival Chief & More From Lido More from this Story Arc Stuart Fails To Save The Universe July 23, 2026 ‘Stuart Fails To Save The Universe’ Creators & Stars On Premiere’s Shocking OG Death & Its Aftermath July 23, 2026 ‘Stuart Fails To Save The Universe’ Panel Teases DC Episode, OG Returns As ‘Big Bang Theory’ Franchise Makes Comic-Con Return View All Story Arc Read More About: Comments

title: John Leguizamo “Could Not See” For 12 To 14 Hours A Day Playing Blind ‘The Odyssey’ Role
full_text: Skip to main content By Glenn Garner Glenn Garner Associate Editor @xoxo_glenn More Stories By Glenn Cole Hauser On “Challenges” Of Making ‘Dutton Ranch’ Without Taylor Sheridan: “Everything Had Changed” John Leguizamo “Could Not See” For 12 To 14 Hours A Day Playing Blind ‘The Odyssey’ Role ‘Obsession’s Michael Johnston Wants To Play “Intentional, Meaningful Queer Role”: “Maybe I’ll Write It Myself” View All July 23, 2026 6:01pm John Leguizamo in 'The Odyssey' (2026) Melinda Sue Gordon/Universal Pictures/Courtesy Everett Collection As John Leguizamo joined the cast of Christopher Nolan’s The Odyssey , he found himself immersed in the role in one major way. The Golden Globe nominee recalled that he “could not see” for up to 14 hours a day on the set of the blockbuster, which is now playing in theaters, due to the contact lenses he had to wear in portraying Odysseus’ (Matt Damon) blind servant Eumaeus. “Not being able to see for 12 to 14 hours a day, like I couldn’t see my phone, I couldn’t read, I couldn’t call,” he told Entertainment Tonight . “My other eye wasn’t clear either. I could not see. I had to be taken to the bathroom, I had to be brought back. I had to be taken to eat.” Related Stories News ‘The Odyssey’: First Reactions From Critics And Fanboys News ‘The Odyssey’ Continues To Cut Box Office Swath With $18M+ U.S. Wednesday, 2nd Best For Christopher Nolan Leguizamo added, “When I was alone, I just had to be inside my head because that’s all I had. Too much time with my own thoughts. It’s a dangerous thing.” Watch on Deadline An adaptation of one of the first stories in human history, The Odyssey follows Odysseus (Damon) through the incredible trials and mythical encounters he faces on his decade-long journey home to wife Penelope (Anne Hathaway) and son Telemachus (Tom Holland) after the Trojan War. View this post on Instagram The film also stars Zendaya, Robert Pattinson, Charlize Theron, Lupita Nyong’o, Jon Bernthal, Benny Safdie, John Leguizamo, Elliot Page, Himesh Patel, Bill Irwin, Samantha Morton, Jesse Garcia, Will Yun Lee, Rafi Gavron, Shiloh Fernandez, Mia Goth, Travis Scott and more. title: ‘72 Hours’ Review: Kevin Hart Throws Himself Into the Slapstick but Netflix’s Intergenerational Farce Squanders Its Premise
full_text: × Skip to main content By Frank Scheck Plus Icon Frank Scheck View All July 23, 2026 6:00pm From left: Marcello Hernández, Ben Marshall, Kevin Hart, Kam Patterson and Mason Gooding in '72 Hours.' Courtesy of Netflix There’s an amusing, insightful movie to be made about the culture clash that would inevitably result between a 40-year-old Millennial and several Gen Z twenty-somethings. But that’s not the new Netflix comedy directed by Tim Story ( Barbershop , Ride Along ) and starring Kevin Hart . Instead, as might have been predicted, 72 Hours aims for the lowest common denominator, with a wildly farcical plotline in which Hart’s character is mistaken at various times for a human trafficker, a cop and a Cuban drug lord. He also gets into a brawl with several women in a moving limousine and finds himself in a smuggler’s boat carrying an illegal contraband haul of “whale dicks.” Add to that Hart’s face swollen to monstrous proportions after being stung by a jellyfish, and if all of this sounds hilarious, then this is the movie for you. Related Stories TV Apple Gets Into the Dating Game: New Series Sends Unlikely Couples to World's Most Remote Locations TV Netflix Drops 'Tires' Season 3 Trailer as Shane Gillis' Star Skyrockets 72 Hours The Bottom Line Feels longer. Release date : Friday, July 25 Cast : Kevin Hart, Marcello Hernández, Mason Gooding, Kam Patterson, Ben Marshall, Kevin Dunn, Zach Cherry, Teyana Taylor, Andy Garcia Director : Tim Story Screenwriters : Jon Hurwitz, Hayden Schlossberg, Kevin Burrows, Matt Mider Rated R, 1 hour 45 minutes The script crafted by a quartet of screenwriters features a reasonably clever premise. Hart plays Joe, a marketing executive whose pitch for a new vodka line falls flat. “You don’t seem to get youth culture anymore,” his boss (Kevin Dunn, doing his usual exasperated thing) tells him, adding that if Joe doesn’t get with the times he’ll be passed over for a promotion. It’s fortuitous, then, that Joe finds himself accidentally included in a group chat with several young men planning a bachelor party in Miami. When he impulsively responds with a cash gift, he’s invited to join the revelers, who have no idea that he could be their dad. Desperate to find out what makes the younger generation tick, Joe decides to go, much to the irritation of his longtime girlfriend ( Teyana Taylor ), who is impatient for a marriage proposal. Cue the would-be yucks as Joe meets up with Nick ( Marcello Hernández ), Hunter (Ben Marshall), Freshman (Kam Patterson) — all three comedians Saturday Night Live recruits — and groom-to-be Mason ( Mason Gooding , Heart Eyes , Scream 7 ) in a palatial Miami mansion. The house turns out to be the former home of a deceased drug kingpin, which leads to dangerous complications as Joe suffers a series of misadventures, including several run-ins with a group of criminals led by the menacing Jaze ( Michael Mando , illustrating the dangers of typecasting after his far more serious performance as Nacho Varga in Better Call Saul ). The film’s unfunny nadir comes when the gangsters force Joe to ingest cocaine, which for some reason transforms him into the heavily accented “Riccardo Montana,” complete with Scarface riffs including “say hello to my little friend.” The gag is repeated later on; Hart and the filmmakers apparently are convinced that his Al Pacino impression is a laugh riot. Any stabs at cultural satire about, say, Gen Z’s addiction to documenting their lives on social media, are subsumed by the crazy action-comedy shenanigans, with the younger cast, all of whom have proven themselves funny on prior occasions, unable to elevate the pedestrian material. Hart tries hard (he always tries hard), throwing himself into the slapstick with his usual determination. The fact that he’s able to wrest a few laughs is a testament to his comic chops and professionalism. There are some semi-amusing cameos along the way, including a brief appearance by Mike Epps as a cop, and another performer whose identity has been deemed a spoiler by Netflix. But both are outshone by a hilarious Andy Garcia , who demonstrates that, under the right circumstances, sometimes serious actors can be the funniest of all. It’s a shame, though, to see Taylor, fresh off her Oscar-nominated turn as a fierce revolutionary in One Battle After Another , reduced to playing the disapproving girlfriend. It feels less a departure than a regression. And, of course there are the obligatory outtakes during the end credits, apparently intended to prove that the actors, at least, found the proceedings hilarious. Full credits Release date: Friday, July 25 Production: Counterbalance Entertainment, Davis Entertainment, Hartbeat Productions, The Story Company Distributor: Netflix Cast: Kevin Hart, Marcello Hernández, Mason Gooding, Kam Patterson, Ben Marshall, Kevin Dunn, Zach Cherry, Teyana Taylor, Andy Garcia Director: Tim Story Screenwriters: Jon Hurwitz, Hayden Schlossberg, Kevin Burrows, Matt Mider Producers: John Davis, Jon Hurwitz, Hayden Schlossberg, Josh Heald, Kevin Hart, Luke Kelly-Clyne, Bryan Smiley, Tim Story, Will Packer Executive producers: Ross Fanger, Vicky Mara Story, Kayla Stamps, John Fox, Jeremy Stein, Dina Hillier, Bill Block, Prescott Ashe Director of photography: Larry Blanford Production designer: Clayton Hartley Costume designer: Salvador Perez Jr. Music: Christopher Lennertz Editor: Peter S. Elliot Casting: Nicole Bruno, LaShawnna Stanley, Mary Vernieu, Michelle Wade Byrd Rated R, 1 hour 45 minutes THR

title: ‘Stuart Fails to Save the Universe’ Co-Creator Bill Prady Explains the Science Behind the Show’s Multiverse
full_text: Jul 23, 2026 6:00pm PT ‘Stuart Fails to Save the Universe’ Co-Creator Bill Prady Explains the Science Behind the Show’s Multiverse By Michael Schneider Plus Icon Michael Schneider Variety Editor at Large franklinavenue Latest ‘Stuart Fails to Save the Universe’ Co-Creator Bill Prady Explains the Science Behind the Show’s Multiverse 10 hours ago Shaking Up the Emmys By Moving Five Categories Out of the Telecast Might Not Work, But at Least the TV Academy Is Trying Something 12 hours ago ‘Breaking Bad’ Star Giancarlo Esposito Signs With UTA for Representation (EXCLUSIVE) 17 hours ago See All HBO Max SPOILER ALERT: This story contains spoilers from the series premiere of “ Stuart Fails to Save the Universe ,” now streaming on HBO Max. HBO Max’s “Stuart Fails to Save the Universe” opens in a post-apocalyptic Pasadena, an alternative world where zombie hands poke out of the ground, giant moths terrorize, random tears in reality serve as vacuum portals to unknown parts of the galaxy and survivors barter with their last remaining canned goods. Related Stories Justin Baldoni Speaks Out on Blake Lively Legal Battle: 'There Have Been So Many Painful Things That Have Been Spoken Into Existence' About the only thing still operating is the comic book shop run by Stuart Bloom (played by Kevin Sussman ), where the gang on “The Big Bang Theory” would sometimes hang out. But even that store is no longer normal, as geeky fights among patrons quickly turn deadly. It’s not a great place, so when another Stuart from a different dimension arrives, so begins our main character’s quest to save the universe. Popular on Variety According to Alt Stuart, it was “The Big Bang Theory” characters Leonard, Sheldon and Howard who built a quantum interference device that caused reality to be altered. Regular Stuart and Bert (Brian Posehn) embark on a quest to find the device at Cal Tech — and along the way, reconnect with Denise (Lauren Lapkus) and discover that Kripke (John Ross Bowie) has turned into an evil gang leader controlling much of the San Gabriel Valley. They also find Raj (Kunal Nayyar), who’s been imprisoned by Kripke for quite some time and is looking to help them fix the device. They’re all captured, but Bert uses his geology expertise to create bombs with rocks and water — and they make it to the device. All is saved, until Kripke kills Raj — and the device then activates, taking Stuart, Bert and Kripke to another universe. So how much of the show’s multiverse can be explained by science? Sussman credits co-creator Bill Prady with having a handle on where the science works. “There’s a bunch of quantum physics involved in the show, and Bill is an expert on quantum physics theory,” Sussman says. “You can get away with a lot because at the end of the day, it is theory. It’s popular theory, but it’s legit. So I would ask him a question like, ‘how can my character be here and be there at the same time?’ And I had an epiphany at one point when I realized, oh wait, so it’s not just parallel universes — it’s parallel universes with a little time travel mixed in!” Sussman says there were points while filming the show where it would help to understand the quantum physics theory behind what was happening in a particular scene. “I look forward to people on Reddit trying to dissect what’s happening because I know that all of it can be argued,” he adds. “I would try to poke holes in it on set, and Bill always walked me through. ‘No, see, the reason why this does make sense is A, B and C.’” Prady explains that the device that Leonard, Sheldon and Howard built seems to have shifted the primary characters to a universe with a separate, distinct history that has an apocalyptic event that occurs sometime after “The Big Bang Theory” and before we meet them. “The Einsteinian issue that time is a fourth dimension comes into play here,” he says. “Because when the device malfunctions, it alters reality, but it creates a new past as well. So when we meet them in the first episode, the apocalypse is some years ago, and that’s a result of the alteration. Both the past and present and the future are altered from what they should have been.” So then the question is, when the characters move between universes, does the universe they left still exist, or is it being restructured? “That is ambiguous, and they don’t know, and we don’t know,” he says. For example — now that Kripke has killed Raj, that doesn’t seem good right? But in Episode 2, he will point out that either the universe they just left doesn’t exist anymore, in which case he didn’t kill Raj because that Raj never existed, or that they’re we are dealing with an infinite number of universes, and so he just killed one Raj out of an infinite number, which is pretty minor. But “the branching theory of multiverses states essentially that every quantum event that could go one way also could go the other, and at that point there’s a branch, both universes exist side by side… The theory is that the universe branches at every decision point, so there’s a rapidly growing, infinite number of universes. That gets into the very annoying realization that some infinities are bigger than other infinities, which is mathematically true and conceptually uncomfortable. But it would state that from the point of the Big Bang, the number of infinite parallel universes increases with every moment that the universe ticks by. So that was kind of the science and pseudoscience that sort of lays this out.” Prady jokingly adds: “How useless is that?” But it’s a reminder that “Stuart Fails to Save the Universe” may be science-fiction, but it’s still grounded in some ways. “When I was teaching at USC, and somebody had a project that involved fantasy elements, I’d say, the most important rule in writing fantasy is that the rules you create for your fantasy have to be as real as the laws of physics. They have to be consistent, and they always have to be the same,” he says. Prady uses “Bewitched” as an example, with its arbitrary rule that only the witch who casts the spell can remove it. “It’s based on nothing, but it keeps every ‘Bewitched’ episode from being three minutes long,” he says. “Because without that rule, Endora would give Darrin donkey ears, and Samantha would remove them, and we’d be done. Now, can your universe be raggedy around the edges? 100%. And that’s ‘Doctor Who.’ The rules are there, but sometimes they don’t quite work out, and the Doctor will explain.” In the case of multiverses, the parameters of what we think of as the “multiverse” has been cemented in pop culture by Marvel films, “Rick and Morty,” “Dark Matter” and more. “I think the reason that you wind up with multiverses with similar stories is that the notion that multiverses exist mathematically. Things like string theory and stuff touch on that. So you can build off of what has sort of percolated through the general consciousness.” Prady notes that not all of it makes sense, and he’s proud of the fact that the characters will acknowledge that. “A guy online did a scientific analysis of what must be happening in this, and where he explains all the various scientific multiverse theories that we are clearly drawing from,” Prady says. “It was thick, thick science. It does state that there’s an infinite number of multiverses. So clearly, we should send that article to HBO Max and say, ‘look, there’s a scientific basis for additional seasons!’” Ubisoft Hires Former Amazon Games Chief Christoph Hartmann as Head of New Division, Including Oversight of Acquired ‘March of Giants’ Game ‘Every Year After’ Renewed for Season 2 at Amazon; Will Focus on Charlie’s Story From ‘One Golden Summer’ Sequel Book Prime Day Deal: Amazon’s New ‘Framed’ Ember Artline 4K TV Is on Sale for Its Lowest Price Ever Amazon Signs Wide-Ranging Deal With Kevin Durant and Rich Kleiman’s Boardroom Spanning Podcasts, Live Events and More Tubi to Provide Creatorverse Lineup to Amazon’s Fire TV (EXCLUSIVE) Amazon Teams With Papa Johns, Beck, Jill Scott, Omar Courtz for New Alexa Ads Using Agentic AI Loading comments... title: ‘Toy Story 5’ Has Given Away 18,000 Toys to Sick Kids
full_text: × Skip to main content By Naomi Jeffries Plus Icon Naomi Jeffries Intern View All July 23, 2026 4:55pm Toy Story's Woody and Jessie visiting a children's hospital. Courtesy of Disney With $957.3 million at the global box office , Toy Story 5 is on track to become the highest-grossing film in the franchise. But the Pixar sequel’s impact isn’t just being measured in ticket sales. Through Disney’s longtime partnership with Starlight Children’s Foundation, the film’s release is also bringing the magic of Woody, Buzz Lightyear and their friends to hospitalized children around the world. Since 2001, Disney and Starlight Children’s Foundation have collaborated to provide hospitalized children with exclusive screenings, care packages and character-inspired experiences. Through its flagship “Disney Movie Moments” program, the company converted hospital spaces into immersive screening events for patients and their families. Related Stories News Harrison Ford Celebrates Michael J. Fox as the "Most Stubborn Optimist I Know" at THR's Social Impact Summit TV 'Percy Jackson' Gets Season 3 Premiere Date As part of the Toy Story 5 rollout, the film became available to patients in more than 400 children’s hospitals globally, including more than 300 across the United States. Disney says it distributed more than 18,000 Toy Story 5 -themed toys and 34,000 character-inspired hospital gowns to children and families across participating hospitals. Woody and Jessie with a patient in a Toy Story-themed hospital gown. Courtesy of Disney Disney also introduced a first-of-its-kind red carpet activation at the film’s Los Angeles premiere , where cast members and guests stopped at a life-size interactive lily pad to write messages of encouragement for children in hospitals. Tim Allen , Greta Lee , Tony Hale , Conan O’Brien, Joan Cusack and other members of the film’s ensemble participated in the activation. Says Hale, “This is amazing! Disney is sending Toy Story to all these fantastic kids in hospitals around the nation.” The heartfelt messages were shown at hospitals in Washington, D.C., Oakland, and Orlando with another immersive Disney hospital experience. Children were surprised with visits from Woody and Jessie, Toy Story -themed gift packages and drawing sessions led by Pixar animators. Conan O’Brien (Smarty Pants) and Joan Cusack (who voices Jessie, far right) at the Toy Story 5 L.A. premiere. Emma McIntyre/Getty Images “ Toy Story has always celebrated the connections that bring us together, and the cast, crew and premiere guests carried that spirit onto the red carpet by writing personal messages for children in hospitals before the world even saw the film.” said Disney’s Lisa Haines, senior vp corporate social responsibility. “Whether it’s a beloved character by a child’s side or a note from the voice of Buzz Lightyear, those simple gestures are incredibly powerful and can create moments of comfort and joy for kids when they’re needed most.” THR

title: Paramount-Warner Bros. Merger Pause Extended
full_text: Skip to main content By Winston Cho Plus Icon Winston Cho View All July 23, 2026 4:35pm Photo by Justin Sullivan/Getty Images Logo text Paramount won’t be able to close its $111 billion takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery until Aug. 18 at the earliest, with the court extending the temporary restraining order another 14 days. U.S. District Judge Araceli Martínez-Olguín issued the extension to allow lawyers for the studio and the states looking to block the deal to come to an agreement on the schedule and format for legal proceedings on a preliminary injunction, which would freeze the transaction until the case is decided if granted. The states alleged that the acquisition will substantially throttle competition in wide-release and top-grossing theatrical distribution and cable licensing in violation of antitrust laws. Separately, the Writers Guild of America filed a lawsuit claiming that the merger will result in lower compensation and worse deal terms. The court on Monday granted the states’ bid to pause the deal. Legal jockeying followed, with Paramount urging the court to hold a three-day evidentiary hearing next month. That would allow it to “address critical factual issues involving market definition, real-world competitive dynamics, barriers to expansion, and incentives,” wrote Jeffrey Kessler, a lawyer for the studio, in a court filing. Paramount intends to put on expert witnesses and cross-examine the states’ experts to undermine arguments that the merger will suppress the markets at issue in the case. It has maintained that the states’ understanding and calculation of the markets are faulty. The states opposed the bid, arguing that a multi-day hearing isn’t appropriate at the preliminary injunction stage and that Paramount is essentially asking for a trial without giving them proper time. For Paramount, the Sept. 30 deadline to close the deal is a key consideration. It previously offered not to consummate the acquisition for up to a month if the court agreed to schedule preliminary injunction proceedings at the end of August in order for a decision to be rendered before the ticking fee is triggered. The ticking fee in the deal contract would result in a $6.9 million per day penalty if the transaction isn’t closed by Sept. 30. The decision on the preliminary injunction will be key. If it’s not granted, Paramount has signaled that it will close, leaving the states to try and unwind the merger. The court will consider arguments on the motion on Aug. 3. THR

title: ‘Every Year After’ Star Joseph Chiu on Season One Reception and Hopes for Next Season
full_text: × Skip to main content By Nicole Fell Plus Icon Nicole Fell Associate Editor nicolemfell Follow Sign Up View All July 23, 2026 4:20pm Joseph Chiu as Jordie. Prime Video “I was screaming in my bedroom when they called me at 10 a.m.,” Every Year After star Joseph Chiu says of getting the news that Prime Video had renewed the romance series for a second season. The 24-year-old Canadian actor plays Jordie Lin in the streamer’s adaptation of the Carley Fortune romance novel Every Summer After , which was released last month. In the weeks after the release, Prime Video announced that Every Year After would be returning for season two and follow Fortune’s follow-up, Our Golden Summer . In Fortune’s book, Chiu’s character, the best friend of Matt Cornett’s leading man, plays a much smaller role, which allowed the actor some space to find his own Jordie. “I was fully committed to the best friend role and doing the best of my abilities to make Sam seem more well-adjusted,” he says. “I really like Stiles from Teen Wolf . There were a lot of references that I was trying to get that energy of in terms of the best friend [character].” Related Stories TV 'Yellowjackets' Star Sophie Nélisse to Lead Netflix Adaptation of Romance Novel 'This Summer Will Be Different' TV A Little Burnout and a Lot of Lakeside Love, How Carley Fortune Became Canada's Summer Romance Darling The actor was fresh from his college graduation when he booked the part, and he wasn’t exactly expecting things to happen so quickly. He found his way into acting during school after he swapped majors. It also wasn’t the first time he’d had a bit of a pivot. When he was 14, he was a K-pop trainee under Korean entertainment company FNC Entertainment. “I was a trainee for three months,” he says. “I actually did pretty well in that program, but then my mom wanted me to finish high school and finish college.” Below, Chiu discusses making season one, what he’s hoping for in season two and how he feels about Canada’s recent film and television spotlight. Were you feeling good after the first season? Were you hopeful? Honestly, with these things, I have no clue. I was on a show called Motorheads where I did a five-episode arc, and it seemed like people were very excited about season two. But then we didn’t get renewed. I was just happy with how season one worked out for me because the way that I was cast was just crazy in itself. I was just grateful to have work as an actor. You auditioned for a different role, correct? You were just graduating from university, right? When did it all hit you? Honestly, I think everything hit me after the show released. Up until then, I was just so obsessed with the whole preparation aspect of the character and really trying to justify the choices that I made with the character. I poured a lot of myself into this role of Jordie. As a 23-year-old still trying to shop for personalities, it was a little difficult and very vulnerable for me, at least, to decide how much of myself I wanted to actually show. This was my first series regular role, and I’ve never been flown out for a role before. I’ve always worked in Toronto or Hamilton. Once I arrived on set, the first day Amy [B. Harris] invited me to just go see the set. I was a little shy, honestly. But then my first scene was actually my audition scene as Jordie. Once I started doing that, it just became fun again. There was zero pressure to get it right just because, specifically for me, Jordie’s not a character really in the book that much. It was just really going moment to moment and really trying to make. I’m just trying to be as present as possible with the two actresses on the day, which was Sadie and Aurora. What was that shooting experience like? What are you hoping for this next season in that regard? Shooting on Bowen Island was beautiful. We took a boat to work every morning. I struggle with allergies, so I was drugged out of my mind on antihistamines. It made me feel a little drowsy, so I drank a lot of black coffee. In terms of the island itself, it’s really beautiful. I think it’s quite similar to Barry’s Bay, although I’ve never been, so I can’t really be saying. Carly seemed happy with the similarities, so we can take her word for that. What I will say, though, is that it reminded me of where my grandmother used to live, which was Wellington, New Zealand. There’s just a sense of peace and boredom. There’s a big sense of boredom. But honestly, that’s what I’ve realized, now in hindsight, that’s a sense of safety. You know how people say touch grass? Yeah. I was touching grass every day for three and a half months, so it was pretty awesome. Where do you think Jordie’s at going forward? I like this question because I can just become the writer now. Let’s hear some of your fan fiction. My PR answer is that that’s a great question for our showrunner, but what I want to prescribe with the power I have, as one of the actors, would just be, I’d like to see Jordie and Chantal in an actual relationship. Aurora and I, we have a certain kind of chemistry where it can become kind of comedic, but then immediately we can go to the depths of seriousness and just really talk about some cool stuff. As for Delilah, I don’t know, I kind of have in real life, there’s a childhood crush and it was unrequited love. I’d like to use that and try to draw on that and maybe build up this moment of confrontation with Delilah of all these feelings that Jordie has buried. The way the ending scene of her looking, it’s exciting for me as an actor because I’m just excited to entertain all of the possibilities in terms of the stakes. Now that Chantal is involved, I do want to explore the backstory between Delilah and Jordie. They live in a small town, but Jordie’s clearly in love with her or was in love, and how Jordie has coped throughout the years. I want to see some jealousy from Jordie too in the past and how he grew into acceptance. Abby’s a really funny actor, so I think we’ll have some good comedic moments balanced out with maybe a big rain confession. I want to see Jordie sing when he was maybe 17. He got really drunk at a bar and starts saying, “Hey There Delilah.” Something like that. I think it’d be really cool. I love that. Also, Delilah coming to watch Jordie play a basketball game and he’s aura farming the whole time. People are really paying attention to the Canadian film and TV industry at this moment, which I have to imagine as a young actor is quite exciting for you as a young actor. It feels great. Alicia and I are the only Canadian actors, but Alicia is based in L.A. But if you book a series regular role on a US production that films in Toronto, your peers literally celebrate you. Because we’re Canada, we really, really put our people in the spotlight and just try to send them to go further down to Hollywood, so it’s actually insane. It’s everything I could have dreamed of. Now, for me, it’s just about celebrating my friends who are also in pursuit of this career and trying to uplift them and their talent. As Canadians, we don’t really have. The access is less. It’s less spotlight, so I’m just trying to use my platform currently to just to celebrate my friends and also people that I really admire. THR

title: Peacock’s ‘Dungeon Crawler Carl’ Series Casts Audiobook Narrator Jeff Hays
full_text: Skip to main content By Rick Porter Plus Icon Rick Porter Television Business Editor rickporter Follow Sign Up View All July 23, 2026 4:00pm Jeff Hays Hampton Stevens Logo text Peacock ‘s Dungeon Crawler Carl series has cast its first actor — one who will be familiar to those who have listened to the audiobook versions of Matt Dinniman’s novels. Jeff Hays, the narrator and producer of the DCC audiobooks, will voice the character of Princess Donut in the live-action series. Princess Donut is a key character in the books, a cat belonging to Carl’s ex-girlfriend who acquires the ability to speak when they become part of a survival game show run by the aliens who have destroyed most of humanity. Hays joined Dinniman and Matt Yost, writer and executive producer of the Peacock series, on stage Thursday at Comic-Con to announce his casting. Related Stories Movies Johnny Depp Surprises Comic-Con as Ebenezer Scrooge on Streets of San Diego TV New 'Avatar' Animated Series at Paramount+ Lands Fall Premiere Date, Releases Teaser Peacock placed a series order for Dungeon Crawler Carl in June. The show comes from Universal Global Television and Seth MacFarlane’s Fuzzy Door Productions, which hold the rights to Dinniman’s book series. Yost ( Maul: Shadow Lord ) is executive producing with MacFarlane, Erica Huggins and Rachel Hargreaves-Heald for Fuzzy Door. Dinniman is a co-exec producer. Here’s the full description of the series: “An alien invasion has wiped out most of humanity and any survivors are forced to fight for their lives on a sadistic intergalactic game show. Sounds bad, right? Now try doing it with bare feet and a stuck-up, self-centered, tiara-wearing talking cat as your partner. Welcome to Dungeon Crawler World: Earth, where the apocalypse will be televised … and Coast Guard vet Carl finds himself stuck with his ex-girlfriend’s award-winning show cat, Princess Donut the Queen Anne Chonk, as they try to survive the end of the world, fighting monsters, aliens, an insane A.I. and even other survivors … all for the sake of good TV. Survival is optional. Entertainment is not.” The Dungeon Crawler Carl audiobooks have logged more than 140 million listening hours on Audible to date. Hays narrates and does voices for the characters, including Princess Donut. He is repped by Matthew N. Sugarman at Weintraub Tobin. THR

title: Devon Allman Says “Hot Damn” To Landing First Major Acting Role In AMC’s NASCAR Drama ‘Thunder Road’
full_text: Skip to main content By Rosy Cordero Rosy Cordero Associate Editor, TV @SocialRosy More Stories By Rosy Devon Allman Says “Hot Damn” To Landing First Major Acting Role In AMC’s NASCAR Drama ‘Thunder Road’ ‘BMF’ Star Da’Vinchi Joins ‘Chicago Fire’ For Season 15 Natasha Rothwell Joins Hulu’s Cable Guy Pilot View All July 23, 2026 5:33pm Devon Allman Madison EXCLUSIVE: Devon Allman is headed to Hollywood. The musician has been cast in AMC ‘s NASCAR drama series Thunder Road from creator John Fusco ( Young Guns, The Highwaymen ). He will star opposite Dennis Quaid, Chase Stokes, Michael Rooker, Matt Barr, Maggie Grace and West Duchovny. The series is set to begin production this summer and will debut on AMC and AMC+ in 2027. Related Stories Documentary ‘Gregg Allman: The Music of My Soul,’ Documentary On Rock Music Legend, Gets Theatrical Release Date News Dickey Betts Dies: Allman Brothers Guitarist, Singer & Songwriter Was 80 Thunder Road is the multi-generational saga of the fictional Whitlock family, whose legacy in stock car racing is as deep as the family’s ties to the Southern hill country roots that shaped them. Quaid stars as racing legend Duane Whitlock, aka The Wrecking Ball, a towering figure who built a racing empire from a legacy of moonshine runs and dirt tracks and refuses to let it die. With ruthless competitors who will stop at nothing to dethrone and destroy them, the Whitlocks are a blue-collar dynasty at the edge of extinction – and the old king is fighting to hold onto the crown. Watch on Deadline In a recurring role, Allman will play Jimmy Fuchs, a silver-tongued Southern operator whose easy charm and “sportsman’s club” credentials barely conceal the danger underneath. “Hot damn! Being cast in AMC’s Thunder Road is such a thrill,” shared Allman. “I’m excited to act alongside this incredible cast and bring the character of Jimmy Fuchs to life. He’s quite an ornery cuss, and I am right here for every bit of it.” Added Fusco: “We couldn’t be more thrilled to welcome Devon Allman to Thunder Road . As the son of Southern rock icon Gregg Allman, Devon carries a legacy in his blood — but he’s a gifted performer and a magnetic presence entirely in his own right. That’s exactly the alchemy this show runs on. In a story world that mines deep family legacy at a crossroads, Devon brings a lived-in authenticity you simply can’t manufacture. He doesn’t play the next-gen South — he’s of it. As Jimmy Fuchs, he’s going to be electric.” Fusco is set to executive produce alongside showrunner Taylor Elmore, Cliff Roberts with Syndicate Entertainment, Mark L. Smith, and NASCAR Studio’s Tim Clark and John Dahl. Gladys Cheng serves as producer. The show is produced in association with NASCAR. title: ‘Stuart Fails To Save The Universe’: Here Is Why Episodes Of ‘Big Bang Theory’ Spinoff Are So…
full_text: Skip to main content By Nellie Andreeva Nellie Andreeva Editor-In-Chief @DeadlineNellie More Stories By Nellie ‘Stuart Fails To Save The Universe’ EPs & Composer On Show’s Ever-Changing Title Sequence You “Shouldn’t Skip”, Packed With Messages & Spoilers ‘Stuart Fails To Save The Universe’ Creators & Stars On Premiere’s Shocking OG Death & Its Aftermath ‘Stuart Fails To Save The Universe’ Panel Teases DC Episode, OG Returns As ‘Big Bang Theory’ Franchise Makes Comic-Con Return View All July 17, 2026 2:05pm Story Arc (L-R): Brian Posehn, John Ross Bowie, Lauren Lapkus, Kevin Sussman in 'Stuart Fails To Save the Universe' HBO Max Let’s hope the writers and producers of HBO Max’s upcoming Big Bang Theory spinoff Stuart Fails To Save the Universe are not paid by the minute for what they have made. Not shackled by broadcast schedules where half-hour comedies need to be exactly 22 minutes to fit into a 30-min slot with time for ads, episodes of streaming comedies typically run between 25 min – 35 min. Not Stuart Fails To Save the Universe. Despite the fact that they do not have time constraints, the show’s creators, Chuck Lorre , Bill Prady and Zak Penn , went in the other direction with episodes that are very short, even by broadcast standards. Related Stories News 'Stuart Fails To Save The Universe' Creators & Stars On Premiere's Shocking OG Death & Its Aftermath News 'Stuart Fails To Save The Universe' EPs & Composer On Show's Ever-Changing Title Sequence You "Shouldn't Skip", Packed With Messages & Spoilers Of the 10 episodes of Season 1, one is 15-minutes long, four are 18 min with the other five at 20 min, 21 min, 22 min, 24 min and 25 min. Why was that? With the elaborate sets and special effects involved to create the different alternate realities in each episode, did the producers run out of money in the budget? “No, no. We just felt that we’d successfully told the story, and to add would be padding, and padding, generally, is a hammock between important scenes, and why would we do that if there’s no real reason,” Lorre said. He and Prady have spent most of their careers working in broadcast comedy, creating episodes to fit the 22-min template with breaks for commercials. “The historic length of a show is entirely arbitrary, it comes from network television where a certain amount of advertising has to be sold to pay the way of making this television, but it’s not an element any longer in streaming,” Lorre said. “So, why not just make the best show we can and hope the audience is entertained and is not looking at their watch, going, ‘Oh, I need five more minutes. You need to go further with this.’ I don’t think that would happen, that’d be my guess. I would think you either loved the episode or you didn’t, and you weren’t looking at your watch.” (You can watch the video below.) This is Lorre’s third single-camera streaming comedy after the Golden Globe-winning The Kominsky Method for Netflix and Bookie for HBO Max. He has adjusted well to the binge-friendly format, with each episode of Stuart Fails To Save the Universe ending with the heroes landing in a new alternate reality to tease what comes next. “The way we structured it, it just fell into our laps, it’s a teaser for the next week but it also felt like a natural way of telling this story,” Lorre said. Added Prady, “We found it in the first episode where we wanted to go, ‘Everything’s all right. Oh wait, no, it isn’t.’ And that was fun, so we kept doing it.” Stuart Fails to Save the Universe premieres on HBO Max July 23 with episodes released weekly. title: ‘Stuart Fails to Save the Universe’ Creator Says ‘Big Bang’ Character Was “Worst Choice” To Lead…
full_text: Skip to main content By Glenn Garner Glenn Garner Associate Editor @xoxo_glenn More Stories By Glenn Jon Bon Jovi Abruptly Ends MSG Show Amid Vocal Issues, Will Reschedule: “You’re Not Getting The Best Of Me” Cole Hauser On “Challenges” Of Making ‘Dutton Ranch’ Without Taylor Sheridan: “Everything Had Changed” John Leguizamo “Could Not See” For 12 To 14 Hours A Day Playing Blind ‘The Odyssey’ Role View All July 22, 2026 5:45pm Story Arc Kevin Sussman in 'Stuart Fails to Save the Universe' Colin Remas Brown/HBO Max With the Big Bang Theory spin-off machine operating in full force after nearly 20 years, series co-creator Chuck Lorre explained the choice behind the franchise’s latest lead. While discussing Stuart Fails to Save the Universe , premiering Thursday at 9pm ET on HBO Max, Lorre explained that the titular comic book store owner was “the absolute worst choice” for the offshoot as he was a minor character that nobody would expect to step up and save the world. “He was the absolute worst choice to be the hero of this story, which made him the perfect choice,” Lorre told People . “And Kevin Sussman , as I’m sure you know, is just a brilliant comic actor, someone you can root for despite his inadequacies. You can look for him.” Related Stories News ‘Stuart Fails To Save The Universe’ Stars Kevin Sussman & Lauren Lapkus Talk Sci-Fi ‘Big Bang’ Spinoff With “Reluctant Hero” At Center & Romance News ‘Big Bang Theory’ Alum Joins Spinoff ‘Stuart Fails To Save The Universe’ Lorre noted that “it was always Kevin” he had in mind for the series, adding, “And right from the very beginning it was Kevin, and the comic book story was.” Watch on Deadline Sussman previously admitted to Deadline that leading his own spin-off “ was not on my bingo card ” as a working actor. “I was not expecting this,” he said. Kevin Sussman and Chuck Lorre attend the premiere of ‘Stuart Fails to Save the Universe’ on July 21, 2026 in Los Angeles, California. (Presley Ann/Getty Images for HBO) In Stuart Fails to Save the Universe , Stuart Bloom (Sussman) must stop a “multiverse armageddon” after accidentally damaging a device created by Sheldon Cooper and Leonard Hofstadter. The adventure leads Stuart and his friends to encounter alternate versions of themselves across other universes. The spin-off, from co-creators Lorre, Zak Penn and Bill Prady, sees Stuart reunite with his pals Bert (Brian Posehn), Denise (Lauren Lapkus) and Kripke (John Ross Bowie). title: ‘Stuart Fails To Save The Universe’ Panel Teases DC Episode, OG Returns As ‘Big Bang Theory…
full_text: Skip to main content By Nellie Andreeva Nellie Andreeva Editor-In-Chief @DeadlineNellie More Stories By Nellie ‘Stuart Fails To Save The Universe’ EPs & Composer On Show’s Ever-Changing Title Sequence You “Shouldn’t Skip”, Packed With Messages & Spoilers ‘Stuart Fails To Save The Universe’ Creators & Stars On Premiere’s Shocking OG Death & Its Aftermath ‘Stuart Fails To Save The Universe’ Panel Teases DC Episode, OG Returns As ‘Big Bang Theory’ Franchise Makes Comic-Con Return View All July 23, 2026 5:33pm Story Arc (L-R) Kevin Sussman, Lauren Lapkus and John Ross Bowie speak at HBO Max's "Stuart Fails To Save The Universe" Hall H panel during 2026 Comic-Con International: San Diego at San Diego Convention Center on July 23, 2026 in San Diego, California. Amy Sussman/Getty Images Eight years after the cast of The Big Bang Theory made their last Comic-Con appearance ahead of the hit CBS sitcom’s 12th and final season, four Big Bang actors were back at the comic book convention with spinoff Stuart Fails To Save the Universe . The single-camera offshoot’s leads, Kevin Sussman (Stuart), Lauren Lapkus (Denise), John Ross Bowie (Kripke) and Brian Posehn (Bert) were joined on the Hall H stage by series creators Chuck Lorre and Bill Prady, who also co-created Big Bang , and Zak Penn. During a panel after a screening of Stuart Fails To Save the Universe ‘s premiere episode, Prady promised more appearances by Big Bang alums. Related Stories Trailers Johnny Depp Crashes Comic-Con Hall H Panel As Scrooge To Reveal 'Ebenezer' Trailer: "It's Good To Be Back" Casting 'Batman: Caped Crusader' Season 2 Adds Matthew Needham To Voice The Joker “Well, you saw one wonderful member of the the Big Bang Theory family in that first episode,” he said. “There are more, and there are also some performers who are some wonderful minor characters that are coming back.” Watch on Deadline A number of them are in the show’s official trailer , released last month. Making a “drive-by” appearance at the panel was DC Comics’ Publisher and Chief Creative Officer Jim Lee who teased a DC-focused episode of Stuart Fails To Save the Universe. “They requested some DC characters to be involved in an episode,” he said. We saw a glimpse of that in the trailer, which features Mr. Freeze. “It was just amazing that they were trying to do something that was true to our characters, but at the same time find comedy in it,” Lee continued. “What was funny is we had scheduled the set visit to come to the set, and I just see the dialogue, the script. And when I came to the set, I was just blown away. It was completely not what I was expecting, I was expecting a multi-cam setup where they were sitting around, and then they start showing the visual effects that they were going to do, and all the bespoke elements they added to [Stuart’s comic book] store.” Added Lee, “Then Bill start talking about the overall arc of the 10 episodes, and I thought this is super ambitious, this is completely not what I expected. It’s like nerds going through Gulliver’s Travels , and I was sold. I was like, this is awesome, this is fantastic.” Lee closed out with praise for how the show handles the DC IP. “Some of the DC characters they have in it, I think are some of the best versions I’ve seen of those characters, and so I’m super excited for you guys,” he said. Inspired by the set visit, Lee created the cover for an exclusive Stuart Failed to Save the Universe comic book, which is also featured on the show. DC’s Jim Lee made a surprise appearance in Hall H at #SDCC to tease some DC comic book characters who audiences will see in ‘Stuart Fails to Save the Universe,’ which he says are “some of the best” versions of these characters he’s seen. pic.twitter.com/2EOpismjHi — Deadline (@DEADLINE) July 23, 2026 Elsewhere on the panel, Lorre said that the goal with Stuart Fails To Save the Universe was to “try to make a science-fiction comedy that the characters of The Big Bang Theory would watch and argue about.” Questioning reality after his experience on the show, star Sussman spoke about being summoned by Lorre in 2022 for a meeting where the comedy uber producer pitched him the idea for Stuart Fails To Save the Universe. “That was nice of him to make it seem like I might say no,” Sussman quipped. ‘Stuart Fails to Save the Universe’ star Kevin Sussman jokes that the many iterations of the Comic Center of Pasadena are starting to make him question reality. “Are you real?” he asks Hall H at #SDCC before reminiscing on the early days of the spinoff’s conception. pic.twitter.com/18vCKXUoNx — Deadline (@DEADLINE) July 23, 2026 In Stuart Fails To Save the Universe, which premieres July 23 on HBO Max, comic bookstore owner Stuart Bloom (Sussman) is tasked with restoring reality after he breaks a device built by Sheldon and Leonard, accidentally bringing about a multiverse Armageddon. Stuart is aided in this quest by his girlfriend Denise (Lapkus), geologist friend Bert (Posehn), and quantum physicist/all-around pain in the ass Barry Kripke (Bowie). Along the way, they meet alternate-universe versions of characters we’ve come to know and love from The Big Bang Theory. As the title implies, things don’t go well. Katie Campione contributed to this report. title: trailer forResident Evil
full_text: Skip to main content By Matt Grobar , Patrick Hipes July 23, 2026 9:19am UPDATED with new trailer: Ahead of a Comic-Con Hall H appearance today by Zach Cregger , Sony has dropped a new trailer for Resident Evil , the Weapons helmer’s take on the iconic video game and the six-film franchise that started back in 2002. This comes as the new movie, from Columbia Pictures and Constantin Film in association with TriStar Pictures, preps for its September 18 release in theaters. Related Stories News Austin Abrams In “A Seriously F*cked-Up Situation” In First Look At Zach Cregger’s ‘Resident Evil’ – CinemaCon News Comic-Con 2026 Movie & TV Panel Schedule: Apple TV Debuts In Hall H, ‘Percy Jackson’ Kicks Things Off The new pic follows Bryan ( Austin Abrams ), a medical courier headed for a drop-off at Raccoon City General hospital who unwittingly finds himself in an action-packed, non-stop race for survival as one fateful, horrifying night collapses around him in chaos. Zach Cherry, Kali Reis and Paul Walter Hauser also star. Cregger co-wrote the script with Shay Hatten. Watch on Deadline Check out the trailer above. PREVIOUSLY, April 30: Sony has dropped the first teaser trailer for their Resident Evil film from Weapons and Barbarian helmer Zach Cregger that was previewed in room only at CinemaCon earlier this month. Here it is: The trailer finds star Austin Abrams trudging through a snowy night and up to a dark, empty house. He had a problem on the road, he says, and wants to use the phone. Calling his girlfriend from inside, he says, “Some things have happened,” he’s in “a seriously f*cked-up situation,” and “we might not get to talk to each other again.” We later hear the screech of creatures and a view at one large one who resembles Stellan Skarsgård’s Baron Vladimir Harkonnen from Denis Villeneuve’s Dune films, along with shots of bloodied zombies, an overturned vehicle, bodies falling and exploding bloodily in the snowy street, and more. Abrams’ character is Bryan, a medical courier who unwittingly finds himself in a nonstop race for survival as one fateful, horrifying night collapses around him in chaos. The film marks the latest big-screen adaptation of Resident Evil , the popular horror video game series from Capcom, which has translated into one of the highest-grossing video game-based movie franchises ever. In his appearance at CinemaCon in Las Vegas, Cregger said the film is “a big, scary ride” he hoped would be “true to the spirit of the games.” In comparison to his past films, he said, his take on Resident Evil will have “no narrative acrobatics, time jumps or disorienting chapter things.” Rather, viewers will be “locked in with a protagonist on a foot journey through a world hell-bent on destroying them.” Launched in 1996, the Resident Evil video game franchise centers on the outbreak of a deadly virus tied to the shadowy Umbrella Corporation. Milla Jovovich led the original six-film series, which launched in 2002 and led to more than $1.2 billion in worldwide box office grosses. More recently, Sony returned to the world of Resident Evil with the Johannes Roberts-helmed 2021 reboot Welcome to Raccoon City. Coming off of Weapons — a critical and commercial smash for Warner Bros. that earned Amy Madigan an Oscar and grossed over $269M worldwide — Cregger directed the new Resident Evil from a script written with Shay Hatten. Constantin Film produced the pic and co-financed. The cast also includes Zach Cherry, Kali Reis and Paul Walter Hauser. Producers included Robert Kulzer, Cregger, Roy Lee, Miri Yoon, Carter Swan and Asad Qizilbash. Executive producers are Oliver Berben, Victor Hadida, Richard Wright and Robert Bernacchi. From Columbia Pictures and Constantin Film, in association with TriStar Pictures, Resident Evil hits theaters September 18.
